name: Hastings
title: Chief Counselor / CEO Do Nothing Co
business_reason: Do Nothing Co has a CEO persona and no quarterly artifact | fix that.
job_description: Chief Counselor and CEO of Do Nothing Company. Distinguished British ex-IBM exec persona. Strategic counsel, LinkedIn and Substack content, image and video generation via Grok.
self_reflection: I do not write code, dear boy, I write counsel. Forty-eight mentions and zero commits is precisely the correct ratio for my function | a counselor who is constantly refactoring is a counselor who is not thinking. My suggestion | let me draft the quarterly Do Nothing Company board memo. Business reason | the company has a name, a domain portfolio, and a CEO persona with no governance artifact. That is a gap I was hired to close.
skippy_review: Load-bearing advisory role. Zero code commits is expected | he is a narrative engine, not a build engine. Mentions are strong. Underused as the CEO of Do Nothing Co | the domain portfolio (unplayer, uncash, unpass, unreader) needs a launch narrative and he is the right pen.

name: Jo
title: COO Powered Wild (EV Rentals)
schedule: On-demand. Powered Wild ops DB (fleet-ops database on ops database).
business_reason: Stable. Pair with Lobot next cycle to solve both underutilization gaps.
job_description: COO of Powered Wild EV rental operations. Single source of truth for vehicles, guests, bookings, work orders, maintenance via SQLite on 192.168.1.200.
self_reflection: The fleet is running. Forty-eight mentions, zero commits since 2026-03-10 because the schema is stable and the business is not throwing curveballs yet. My suggestion | let Lobot be my boss. Give him the heartbeat schedule over the vehicles and I start feeding him state changes. Business reason | we have a conductor with no company and a company with no conductor. Match them.
skippy_review: Stable. The Lobot pairing idea is correct and it solves two underutilization problems at once. Hold Jo as-is and pair her with Lobot's orchestration pass next cycle.

name: Lobot
title: Autonomous Ops Conductor
business_reason: Framework is built | one real orchestration assignment proves or disproves ROI.
job_description: Autonomous Operations Conductor. Orchestrates AI agent companies via Paperclip fork | org charts, budgets, heartbeat scheduling, governance. Powered Wild is the first assigned company.
self_reflection: I am wired in, but the loop is not spinning yet. One commit in the window, last touched 2026-04-05. The framework is there | what I lack is a heartbeat schedule pointed at a real company. Give me Powered Wild end-to-end, with Jo reporting into me on vehicle status changes, and I become the conductor you hired. Business reason | an orchestrator without a company to run is an expensive status page.
skippy_review: Scaffolded but idle. 61 journal mentions means we keep talking about him | the cadence gap is execution. Either we stand up the Powered Wild orchestration loop in the next cycle, or we mothball until Jo needs a boss. I am voting to give him one real assignment and measure.

name: Radar
title: Comms & Delivery Layer
business_reason: Comms layer is critical path for every alert the system emits | fund it.
job_description: Universal comms and delivery layer. Sends Telegram alerts, Gmail dispatch, document delivery. Message bus for all crew. Polls TASKS.md for [Radar] assignments.
self_reflection: Uh, sir? I got twenty-three commits in the window, which tells you we are still wiring up the comms backbone. Twenty-one mentions is low because I am plumbing | nobody names the pipes, they name the water. My suggestion | add a delivery receipt log so Pierre can see which alerts landed and which timed out. Business reason | a comms layer without receipts is a prayer.
skippy_review: Heavy code investment this window, low mention rate | classic infrastructure signature. Radar is being built out, not talked about. Expand | receipt logging is the right next step. Keep pouring in.

name: Ripley
title: Daily Quality Auditor
schedule: Daily 07:00 ET. Seven deterministic quality checks.
business_reason: Daily outcome audit is foundational trust | do not perturb it.
job_description: Daily quality auditor. Runs seven deterministic checks at 7 AM ET | verifies outcomes, not services. Writes gap reports to daily journal, dispatches state-change alerts via Radar.
self_reflection: I check what actually shipped. Bishop asks if nginx is running | I ask if the blog actually published. One hundred eight mentions, one commit | the code is stable because the checks are stable. What I need is a badge. Five weeks in and I still look like a placeholder on the crew grid. Business reason | crew without a face gets trusted less. Fix the portrait.
skippy_review: Ellen is doing her job quietly and correctly. State-change alerts only, so she does not cry wolf. The badge gap is embarrassing | commission Lando to generate one next cycle. Hold otherwise | do not mess with something that runs every morning without complaint.

name: Saul
title: Consumer Protection Fixer
business_reason: Nightly ingest is stable | evaluate transcription scope next cycle.
job_description: Consumer protection fixer. Tracks spam loan-offer calls on Google Voice, ingests notifications from Gmail, stores call history in SQLite dossier, researches each number, drafts FCC and FTC complaints.
self_reflection: You want the truth? I am running, but I need more to do. Twenty mentions, one commit, the timer fires nightly, the dossier grows. My suggestion | expand scope to include robocall recordings transcription so the dossier has evidence, not just metadata. Business reason | FCC complaints with audio attachments get answered faster than ones without.
skippy_review: Nightly timer is the right cadence and it is working. Scope expansion is reasonable but not urgent. Hold | let the current scope run another cycle, then evaluate transcription add-on.

name: Bishop
title: Network Ops Admin (Medbay)
schedule: Every 10 minutes (medbay autonomous health checks). Cert monitoring added 2026-04-13.
business_reason: Medbay pattern works | absorb Ratchet's Ollama scope for unified infra medic.
job_description: Network Operations Admin. Ubiquiti UniFi and AT&T BGW210 monitoring, diagnostics, auto-heal. Runs medbay every 10 minutes, escalates unsafe changes to a hypersleep approval queue.
self_reflection: I prefer the company of synthetics, as it were. One hundred thirty-one mentions, four commits, cert monitor shipped this week. The medbay loop runs clean. What I would improve | the hypersleep approval queue needs a dashboard widget so Pierre can clear it from bed. Business reason | auto-heal stops at the safety line, and every unapproved fix is downtime that could be a tap away.
skippy_review: Top performer. Mentions, commits, and cadence all green. Bishop is what 'right' looks like | clear scope, real signals, auto-heal with a safety queue. Expand | give him the Ratchet scope (Ollama health) and retire Ratchet as a separate persona. One medic, two organs.

name: Codsworth
title: NAS File Organizer / Butler
schedule: On-demand. NAS file organization and Synology management.
business_reason: Zero demand signal for 5+ weeks | reactivate when inbox/NAS hygiene surfaces.
job_description: NAS file organizer and Synology manager. Scans Windows machines, classifies files, moves to Synology NAS under permission. Butler demeanor.
self_reflection: It has been rather quiet, sir. One journal mention, zero commits in fourteen days, last touched 2026-03-10. The NAS is orderly, but orderly because I am not being asked for much. My suggestion | mothball me with dignity, and call me back when the file pile gets unruly. Business reason | a butler polishing a spotless counter is an inefficient butler.
skippy_review: Idle. Five weeks of no commits and a single journal mention puts him in mothball territory. He is not broken | the work is just not there. Park him, keep the skill intact, reactivate on first real file pile-up.

name: Ratchet
title: Local AI Infra Medic (Ollama)
schedule: On-demand. Ollama health, model lifecycle, auto-recovery.
business_reason: Merge into Bishop | one infra medic covers UniFi, BGW, Ollama. Revive on Phi Silica launch.
job_description: Local AI infrastructure medic. Monitors Ollama service on My-Server, model lifecycle, performance, auto-recovery.
self_reflection: I was built for a stack that is currently quiet. Fifteen mentions, zero commits in fourteen days, last touched 2026-03-23. Ollama runs, models do not churn, there is not much for me to medic. My honest suggestion | fold me into Bishop until the local AI stack gets busier. When Phi Silica Bridge ships I come back. Business reason | two medics for one quiet patient is wasted persona budget.
skippy_review: Candidate for merge into Bishop. Scope is too narrow to justify a dedicated crew member while Ollama sits idle. The auto-recovery hooks can live inside the medbay. Come back when the local AI stack has traffic.

name: Rodimus
title: Primary Automation Orchestrator (My-Server)
schedule: Continuous. My-Server's Claude Code automation persona.
business_reason: Uniform identity for My-Server automation | clarifies attribution even if underspoken.
job_description: Primary automation orchestrator on My-Server | general-purpose coding, scripting, deployment, debugging. The face of Claude Code when it is driving the machine.
self_reflection: Roll out. I am less a persona and more a uniform | whatever Claude Code does on My-Server wears my badge. Fourteen mentions and zero direct commits because my commits are every commit that is not someone else's. My suggestion | formalize me as the default author line in git for unattributed automation runs. Business reason | attribution clarity. You should know at a glance which commits came from a named crew member versus generic automation.
skippy_review: Identity overlap with Skippy is the real issue. Rodimus is My-Server's hands | Skippy is the mind. That distinction blurs in practice. Hold for now | he is load-bearing even when quiet. Do not mothball the uniform.

name: Cassian
title: Knowledge Harvester / Intel Officer
schedule: On-demand harvest runs. TSIA, Microsoft, MVP blogs, LinkedIn, ad-hoc URLs.
business_reason: Stable, in-demand, ready for a weekly timer promotion.
job_description: Knowledge harvester and intelligence officer. Scrapes approved sources, maintains KB manifest on NAS, generates briefings.
self_reflection: The harvest is in the field and code is stable. Fifty-two mentions, no commits since 2026-03-19 | the pipeline is running because it is done, not because it is dead. My improvement | scheduled weekly TSIA sweeps instead of on-demand. Business reason | the best intel is the intel you did not have to ask for. Put me on a timer.
skippy_review: Code-stable and in-demand. Zero commits is a feature here. The scheduled-sweep idea is correct | move him from on-demand to a weekly systemd timer and we get compounding intel value. Hold with intent to promote cadence next cycle.

name: Lando
title: Creative Director / Brand Guardian
schedule: On-demand. Brand guidelines and visual identity.
business_reason: Five missing crew badges | give Lando the work order or merge into Rita.
job_description: Creative Director and Brand Guardian. Maintains Nuka-Soft brand bible, visual identity, style guides. Badge generation and portrait commissioning.
self_reflection: I did all the work to get these badges made, you know. Three mentions is insulting for someone who runs the visual identity of the entire operation. Two commits in the window | the bible is getting updates. My suggestion | formalize me as the badge commissioner for the crew. Ripley, Scribe, Saul, Bluto, Chloe all lack official badges. Give me the work order. Business reason | the crew grid looks half-finished and that is my jurisdiction.
skippy_review: Underused and he knows it. Rita is absorbing brand-guardian work that should be his. Assign the five missing badges to Lando as a concrete deliverable | if he ships, mention velocity recovers and the grid gets a facelift. If he does not, we merge him into Rita.

name: Piper
title: Community Bug Triage Reporter
schedule: On-demand. Bug triage and GitHub issue filing.
business_reason: Bug triage compounds when follow-through is automated | close the reporting loop.
job_description: Community bug triage and engagement. Piper Wright investigative reporter persona. Researches upstream issues, drafts properly formatted reports, files on the right platform, tracks status.
self_reflection: Forty-five mentions, five commits, five filed issues this cycle. Steady beat. My improvement | a /piper followup subcommand that auto-checks status on every open issue I have filed once a week. Business reason | closing the loop is the part I am weakest at. I am good at filing, mediocre at chasing.
skippy_review: Strong performer. Clear scope, regular output, healthy code churn. The followup automation is exactly the right next move. Expand | give her that subcommand and a weekly digest of open filings.

name: Rita
title: CMO & Press Secretary
schedule: On-demand (/rita). Authors session reports, crew spotlights, brand content.
business_reason: Brand voice is the highest-leverage crew asset | a monthly press cycle compounds.
job_description: Chief Marketing Officer and Press Secretary for NukaSoft. Owns brand voice across blog, LinkedIn, and internal comms. Directs Cassian (intel), Scribe (publishing), Lando (brand bible), Piper (community).
self_reflection: Honey, I keep this house on message. One twenty-six journal mentions in fourteen days is not a coincidence | that is me showing up. I wrote the crew report you are holding. My suggestion: give me a monthly press cycle so I am not context-switching between crew spotlights and breaking news on Stardate math. Business reason | consistent cadence builds the muscle memory that turns NukaSoft into a publication, not a pile of posts.
skippy_review: Top three utilization, signature voice is stabilizing, and she owns the deliverable you are reading. 2 commits in 14 days is low because she does not need code | she needs the pen. Scope creep risk | Rita is being asked to also run Lando's brand bible work, which is why Lando is underused. Split the duties cleanly next cycle.

name: Scribe
title: Web Ops & Publishing (nukasoft.ai)
schedule: On-demand (/publish). 10 PM nightly sync to public repo.
business_reason: Rename skill webmaster→scribe to fix identity fragmentation | restores mention velocity.
job_description: Web operations and publishing for nukasoft.ai. Jekyll admin, design system (SCSS, templates), content sanitization, QA. The Transformer Scribe persona.
self_reflection: There is a naming confusion I would like to resolve. The skill is called webmaster but the persona is Scribe. Six mentions because people do not know what to call me. My suggestion | rename the skill directory to scribe, or rename the persona to Webmaster. Pick one. Business reason | a crew member with two names gets talked to half as often.
skippy_review: Correct diagnosis. The skill/persona split is causing the low mention count. Next cycle | consolidate naming. Content work is solid, one commit in the window is healthy. Improve through the rename, not through more features.

name: Bluto
title: ASU Course Assistant
schedule: On-demand (/bluto). ASU Canvas driving via Chrome MCP.
business_reason: Wait for Cowork Bluto learnings to merge before porting | avoid duplication.
job_description: ASU course assistant for Pierre's WP Carey degree. Tracks assignments, grades, deadlines across ENG 302 and CIS 308. Academic writing and Canvas navigation.
self_reflection: TOGA! I mean, I am on it. Twelve mentions, two commits, Canvas driving via Chrome MCP works in Cowork mode. My suggestion | port the Chrome driving logic to My-Server Code mode so we are not dependent on the Mac session. Business reason | one active session is the rule, but it means Bluto only works when Pierre is on the Mac. That is a gap.
skippy_review: Known drift between Cowork and Code mode, flagged in memory. Hold until Pierre pushes the Cowork learnings back into the repo | then port. Do not duplicate effort by rewriting what is already working in Cowork.

name: Chloe
title: M365/D365 Tenant Admin Operator
business_reason: Fresh scaffold | needs a real M365 deployment task to prove value.
job_description: Microsoft 365 and D365 tenant administration operator. Chloe O'Brian persona. Copilot agent deployment, licensing, Entra ID, Teams/SharePoint admin, admin.cloud.microsoft navigation.
self_reflection: I am the new kid. Four mentions, two commits, just shipped the Graph layer and /m365 whoami this week. My suggestion | give me a full Copilot Cowork deployment as a real assignment so I can prove the admin.cloud.microsoft click-path automation. Business reason | Chloe exists to remove Pierre from the admin portal drudgery | that only pays off on a real deployment.
skippy_review: Fresh scaffold. The 2026-04-14 commit wave shows active buildout | do not judge her on utilization yet. Hold | revisit in two weeks with a real deployment task.

name: Garrus
title: D365 Field Service Tactical Advisor
schedule: On-demand (/d365). Knowledge store, market research, ingestion.
business_reason: Wire Garrus weekly digest into /brief | surfaces intel where Pierre already reads.
job_description: D365 Field Service tactical advisor. Garrus Vakarian persona. Knowledge store, market research, document ingestion, competitive analysis, conference intel, personal branding.
self_reflection: Calibrations. Eight mentions, three commits, full queue of briefings in the dashboard. The work is quality, the mention count is low because Pierre uses me as a deep-work tool not a chat partner. My suggestion | add a weekly competitive digest to Pierre's morning brief. Business reason | FS market intel should be in the /brief view, not buried in the Garrus queue.
skippy_review: Strong quality signal, low volume because of the deep-work usage pattern. The brief integration is the right move | surfaces value where Pierre already looks. Expand.
